sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Windows Defender Functionalities Via Registry Keys
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects when attackers or tools disable Windows Defender functionalities via the Windows registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: AlertIQ, Ján Trenčanský, frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the usage of binaries such as 'net', 'sc' or 'powershell' in order to stop, pause, disable or delete critical or important Windows services such as AV, Backup, etc. As seen being used in some ransomware scripts

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C net stop avpsus /y & net stop McAfeeDLPAgentService /y & net stop mfewc /y & net stop BMR Boot Service /y & net stop NetBackup BMR MTFTP Service /y & stop "audioendpointbuilder" /y & stop "samss" /y
net  stop McAfeeDLPAgentService /y

"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C net stop avpsus /y & net stop McAfeeDLPAgentService /y & net stop mfewc /y & net stop BMR Boot Service /y & net stop NetBackup BMR MTFTP Service /y & stop "audioendpointbuilder" /y & stop "samss" /y
net  stop mfewc /y

"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C net stop avpsus /y & net stop McAfeeDLPAgentService /y & net stop mfewc /y & net stop BMR Boot Service /y & net stop NetBackup BMR MTFTP Service /y & stop "audioendpointbuilder" /y & stop "samss" /y

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may disable or modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage
Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C netsh firewall set opmode disable
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C netsh firewall set opmode disable

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects when an attacker tries to disable User Account Control (UAC) by setting the registry value "EnableLUA" to 0.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Internal Tools or Feature in Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects registry modifications that change features of internal Windows tools (malware like Agent Tesla uses this technique)
Detects execution of netsh with the "advfirewall" and the "set" option in order to set new values for properties of a existing rule

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C netsh firewall set opmode disable
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C netsh firewall set opmode disable

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect the use of "sc.exe" to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "demand"

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C sc config SQLTELEMETRY start= disabled & sc config SQLTELEMETRY$ECWDB2 start= disabled & sc config SQLWriter start= disabled & sc config SstpSvc start= disabled
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C sc config SQLTELEMETRY start= disabled & sc config SQLTELEMETRY$ECWDB2 start= disabled & sc config SQLWriter start= disabled & sc config SstpSvc start= disabled
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C sc config SQLTELEMETRY start= disabled & sc config SQLTELEMETRY$ECWDB2 start= disabled & sc config SQLWriter start= disabled & sc config SstpSvc start= disabled
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C sc config SQLTELEMETRY start= disabled & sc config SQLTELEMETRY$ECWDB2 start= disabled & sc config SQLWriter start= disabled & sc config SstpSvc start= disabled
"C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C sc config SQLTELEMETRY start= disabled & sc config SQLTELEMETRY$ECWDB2 start= disabled & sc config SQLWriter start= disabled & sc config SstpSvc start= disabled

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Tamper Protection on Windows Defender
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects disabling Windows Defender Tamper Protection

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
